channels.creator: U_VREYES

messages.text: Team — fall menu goes live tomorrow. Tony has done an incredible job on the new dishes. Highlights: pumpkin ravioli with brown butter sage, braised lamb shank, and a pear-gorgonzola salad. Full staff tasting today at 3 PM. Know the menu cold by service tomorrow.
Fall special tonight: pumpkin ravioli, brown butter sage, toasted pepitas, shaved Parm. 25 portions prepped. Call your counts loud — this one is going to fly.
86 pumpkin ravioli after 22 portions. Sold out by 8:15. Tomorrow we double prep. Good problem to have.
Reservation notes for tonight: Table 6 is a birthday (guest: Michael), Table 12 is an anniversary — comp desserts flagged in Toast for both. Walk-in 8-top called ahead for 7:30. Big Friday ahead.
Sysco delivery was 30 minutes late today. We were short on mozzarella until the truck showed. Had to stall on the caprese app for a few tables. Dan said it was a routing issue.

Q4 game plan: fall menu is performing well, holiday party inquiries are starting to come in. Let's target $40K in catering deposits by end of November. Carmen — how's the FOH schedule looking for December? Daniela — start compiling the private event calendar. Tony — I need the holiday prix fixe finalized by mid-November.

New catering inquiry: Johnson Corporation annual holiday dinner. ~80 guests, early May 2026. Multi-course seated dinner with wine pairings and cocktail reception. I'm putting together a proposal this week. This could be a big one.
Friday close went smooth. Register balanced, all side work done, floor mopped. I locked up at 11:40. Good night — 195 covers.

Halloween event: we're doing a special prix fixe on Friday the 31st with themed cocktails and desserts. Maria is creating a "Blood Orange Negroni" and Tony has a black squid ink pasta on the menu. Costumes encouraged for staff (keep it work-appropriate). Let's have fun with this.
Mise en place done for Halloween special. Squid ink pasta portioned, lobster for the bisque thawed, dessert prep started. Ready for tomorrow.

Halloween cocktail menu printed and behind the bar. Blood Orange Negroni, Witch's Brew (mezcal, chartreuse, lime), and a Smoked Apple Cider for the non-adventurous. Garnishes prepped. Let's crush it tonight.
Halloween night: 210 covers, squid ink pasta sold out by 9, lobster bisque was the star. Kitchen ran clean. Great energy tonight. Nice work team.

Thanksgiving week schedule: we are CLOSED Thursday (Thanksgiving Day) and will run reduced service on Friday (dinner only, 5 PM – 10 PM). Saturday and Sunday normal hours. Enjoy the day with your families. You all deserve it.

Holiday party bookings status: 6 private events confirmed for December, 3 more pending. NYE prix fixe is going on sale this week. Tony — holiday menu finalized? Carmen — do we have enough staff for the December weekends or do we need to bring in temps?

December staffing looks tight but manageable. I can cover extra closes on the weekends. Jessica and Ryan can handle the additional sections if we stagger their breaks. I'll put together a draft schedule by Friday.

Baird Financial corporate retreat dinner inquiry — ~60 guests, early June 2026. Multi-course seated dinner with full bar. This is a premium client. I'm working on the proposal. Tony, can you draft a suggested menu?

Holiday cocktail menu is live. New additions: Spiced Pear Martini, Cranberry Prosecco Spritz, and a Gingerbread Espresso Martini. Samples at the bar for anyone who wants to taste before tonight's service.

Holiday prix fixe finalized: amuse-bouche (truffle arancini), first course (butternut squash bisque), entrée choice (braised short rib or lobster ravioli), dessert (chocolate torte with salted caramel). Tony tested everything twice. This is our best prix fixe yet.

Heads up FOH: holiday party season starts this weekend. We have a 40-top private event Friday and a 55-top Saturday. Both in the private dining room. Regular dining room will be busy too. Plan your sections accordingly and pre-bus constantly.

Double service tonight — private event in the PDR plus full dining room. Luis, you're on the private event plating. Marco, you're running sauté for the main room. I'll float between both. Let's stay sharp.
86 lobster ravioli after 4 portions — Sysco shorted us on the lobster meat. Switching to mushroom ravioli for the rest of service. Tell your tables.

New Year's Eve prix fixe tickets are on sale. $135/person, 5-course menu with champagne toast. Seating at 6 PM and 9 PM. This will sell out — spread the word to your regulars. All hands on deck that night.
Can I swap my Friday PM shift this week? Something came up. Happy to pick up Sunday brunch instead.
Jessica — approved. You're on Sunday brunch 10:30–5. I'll cover your Friday with the existing team.
December is absolutely crushing it. We did $58K last week — best week of the year. Kitchen is holding up but the team is getting tired. Can we do a staff appreciation meal before NYE? I'll cook.

NYE menu prep starts tomorrow. 5-course tasting: amuse (burrata with truffle honey), oysters Rockefeller, lobster bisque, prime rib with bone marrow butter, and a champagne panna cotta. Marco — you're on oysters. Luis — bisque. Ricky — I need you all night on dish.
NYE bar is set. Champagne flutes polished, Prosecco chilled, cocktail garnishes prepped. Maria and I have the midnight toast choreographed — every table gets a pour at 11:58. Let's make it magic.

Incredible NYE, team. 210 covers across both seatings, zero complaints, kitchen ran perfectly. Bar revenue was our best single night ever. I'm proud of every one of you. Take a well-deserved rest this week. Reduced hours through January 5.
Post-holiday deep clean starts Monday. Walk-in reorganization, hood system degreasing, line equipment teardown. Marco and Ricky — I need you both Monday morning at 8 AM. Let's start the year right.
Walk-in compressor is making a weird noise. Temps are holding at 38 degrees but it sounds like it's working harder than normal. Wanted to flag it.
Thanks Marco. I'll get a tech out this week. Log temps every 4 hours until it's serviced. If it goes above 40, move proteins to the freezer immediately and text me.

Heads up — health inspection estimated for late March or early April based on quarterly cycle. James Okonkwo gave us advance notice. Let's start being audit-ready now: temp logs current, FIFO labels on everything, hand-wash stations fully stocked, all certifications up to date. No surprises.
I'll check all ServSafe certifications this week. Does anyone's expire before July? Let me know so I can schedule renewals.

Kitchen is in decent shape from the holiday deep clean. I'll start the hood filter rotation this month and make sure all FIFO labels are current. Walk-in is tight with fish fry inventory coming but we'll manage.

I'm looking at the register close-out reports from Q4. Some discrepancies that need cleaning up as part of our year-end reconciliation. Nothing unusual — just making sure our books are tight before tax season. Carmen, can you pull the December close reports for me?
Sure, I'll have them for you by end of day tomorrow.
New booking: Alvarez family quinceañera, March 22. Private dining room, 75–100 guests. Family-style Italian. Deposit received ($3,200). Tony — start thinking about a menu. This is a special one.

January slowdown continues. Covers are down ~25% from December, which is normal. I'm adjusting sections — two servers on weeknights, three on weekends. Jessica, you're off Tuesday and Wednesday this week.
Staff meeting this Thursday at 3 PM. Agenda: Q1 planning, health inspection prep, Lenten fish fry kickoff, and spring catering calendar review. Attendance required for all available staff.

Team — Bella Notte is partnering with St. Boniface Church again for the Lenten fish fry series. 7 Fridays starting February 20 through April 3. Extra shifts are posted in Airtable. Thank you to everyone pulling extra hours on fish fry nights.

Fish fry prep plan: 70 portions per week to start, scaling to 85 if demand holds. Menu: beer-battered cod, coleslaw, fries, mac and cheese. Luis — you're on fry station for the series. Let's nail Week 1.

Milwaukee Art Museum fundraiser gala inquiry — April 18, 120–150 guests, Windhover Hall. Cocktail reception + seated dinner. Budget $6,500–$7,500. Caroline Fischer is the contact. I'm scheduling a walkthrough for early March.
Valentine's Day tonight. We're fully booked — 262 covers, both seatings. Prix fixe only. Every table gets a rose. Ryan and Maria have the bar dialed in. Let's give people a night to remember.
Valentine's service is a wrap. 262 covers, no major fires. The beef tenderloin was the star — sold 160 of them. Kitchen ran clean. I'm proud of this team.
Mise en place done for fish fry Week 1. 40 lbs cod portioned, coleslaw prepped, fry oil changed in both fryers. Ready to go.

Fish fry Week 1 was solid. 68 portions sold, no waste. Parish was happy. We need to scale up — Fr. Nowak says word is spreading and he expects more people next week. I'll order 80 portions of cod for Week 2.
Can I swap my Friday PM? I can pick up Saturday lunch or Sunday brunch instead.
Jessica — approved. You're on Saturday lunch. I'll adjust the Friday schedule.

For the UWM Alumni reception — Karen asked about non-alcoholic cocktail options. I'm working on a blood orange spritz (San Pellegrino base, muddled orange, rosemary) and a rosemary-ginger cooler. Samples at the Thursday staff meeting.

Fish fry Week 2 is this Friday. We're scaling to 80 portions. Fr. Nowak says the parish is buzzing — people have been coming from all over the south side. Great for the community and great for Bella Notte's name. Thank you to everyone working the extra Friday hours.
Today's special: pan-seared halibut, lemon caper butter, roasted fingerling potatoes. 20 portions. It's a Friday — this will move fast. Call your counts loud.
86 halibut after 16 portions. Switching to swordfish for the rest of service.

Reservation notes for tonight: Table 4 is a birthday celebration (guest: Sarah), Table 11 is an anniversary — comp desserts flagged in Toast for both. Also a walk-in 6-top called ahead, arriving at 7:30.
Can I swap my Friday shift? Something came up, happy to pick up Sunday instead.
Jessica — approved. I'll update the schedule. You're on Sunday 10:30–5.

Health inspection confirmed: Tuesday, April 1, 10 AM. Inspector is James Okonkwo — same as last quarter. I want everyone on top of their game. Tony — deep clean schedule for this weekend. Daniela — all certifications current? Carmen — make sure the FOH hand-wash stations and signage are perfect.

Kitchen deep clean scheduled: walk-in Saturday morning (Marco and Ricky), hood vents and exhaust Sunday (me), line equipment Monday before service. All FIFO labels will be current by Friday. We got 96 last time. Let's beat it.
All ServSafe certs are current. FOH hand-wash stations will be fully stocked and signage updated by Friday. I'll do a walk-through Saturday.

Alvarez quinceañera is this Saturday (Mar 22). Private dining room, 100 guests. Tony has the menu locked — family-style Italian plus custom appetizer course. Daniela, please confirm the room setup by Friday. Mariachi band arriving 7 PM, rear entrance cleared by 6:45.
Alvarez room setup confirmed — 10 round tables of 8 (plus head table for family), 100 chairs, white linens. Linen & Things delivering Friday. I'll have the room dressed by 4 PM Saturday.

Sysco delivery came 45 min late today. Had to scramble on lunch prep — we were short on mozzarella until the truck showed. Dan said there was a mechanical issue. Might want to keep a buffer if this keeps happening.
Mise en place done for fish fry Friday. 45 lbs cod portioned, coleslaw prepped and chilling, fry oil changed in both fryers. Ready for Week 5.

Happy 3rd anniversary to Bella Notte's partnership with Milwaukee Bread Co.! Anna dropped off extra focaccia today for the team — it's in the back for staff meal. Her bread is a huge part of what makes this place special.
Alvarez quinceañera plating went great tonight. 100 covers in private dining, no issues on timing. Family-style service flowed perfectly. Good work Marco and Luis — smooth as it gets for a Saturday.
Heads up FOH: 3 large parties this weekend. Friday 8-top at 7 PM, Saturday 12-top plus the quinceañera in private dining, Sunday brunch 10-top at 11:30. Plan your sections accordingly.
Copy, thanks Daniela. I'll grab extra side work supplies before my Sunday shift.

Walk-in temp was 42 degrees Saturday afternoon. Marco caught it and we moved proteins to the freezer. Victor called a tech for Monday. Keep the walk-in door closed between grabs — no propping. If you see it over 40, tell me or Victor immediately.

Walk-in hit 42 degrees Saturday afternoon. Marco caught it and we moved perishable proteins to the freezer as a precaution. Compressor relay is being replaced Monday morning — tech confirmed. Everyone log temps twice daily until further notice. This is exactly what Okonkwo looks for. If anyone asks, we caught it, documented it, and fixed it same weekend.
Sunday brunch was smooth. 85 covers. Jessica, great job covering sections 1 through 4 solo during the rush. Table 9's birthday surprise went off without a hitch.

New spring cocktail menu is printed and behind the bar. Maria and I did a full run-through today — all garnishes prepped, all builds dialed in. Highlights: blood orange Negroni, limoncello spritz, and a smoked rosemary old fashioned.

Alvarez event was a success Saturday night. Client was thrilled. Kitchen ran clean, no timing issues, no waste beyond estimate. One note: bar ran low on horchata — for similar events we should prep 50% more.
Lost and found: someone left a navy blue scarf at the host stand last Saturday night. It's in the office. Claim it before Friday or it's going in the donation box.

Working on Dr. Nakamura's tasting menu — 7 courses, Japanese-Italian fusion. Going to need extra prep time the week of April 5. Marco, can you cover sauté that Thursday afternoon so I can focus on plating trials?
Yeah I can cover sauté Thursday. Want me to come in an hour early for setup?

Staff meeting this Thursday at 3 PM before dinner service. Agenda: spring menu launch (April 1), upcoming catering calendar review, April scheduling, and a few housekeeping items. Attendance required for all available staff.
Is the staff meal before or after the meeting Thursday?
Before. I'm making a big batch of carbonara at 2:30. Don't be late or Ricky gets all the guanciale.

Victor — I noticed you pulled the register close-out reports going back a few months. Is there something going on with register procedures I should know about? I've been closing that drawer the same way for two years and I don't appreciate being second-guessed without anyone saying anything to me. If there's a problem with how I'm handling closes, I'd rather you just talk to me directly.

Carmen — nothing to worry about. Routine quarterly reconciliation, same as every year around tax season. I pull reports across all shifts, not just yours. Appreciate you staying on top of the closes — you're one of the most consistent people we have. No issues.

Chen wedding update — Lisa confirmed the menu: bruschetta trio, arancini, butternut squash soup, dual plate (filet + lobster ravioli), tiramisu station with Venetian cookie table. Guest count firm at 180. She wants to add a late-night pizza station around 10 PM. Tony, let's talk logistics for a second kitchen setup on the patio.

Sysco special order placed for the Art Museum gala: 30 lbs beef tenderloin, 20 lbs jumbo shrimp. Delivery scheduled for April 16, two days before the event. Also added extra chafing fuel and disposable serving ware.

Heads up: that Yelp reviewer from the quinceañera weekend posted — 4 stars but mentioned slow service on a Saturday night. Fair point, we were stretched thin. Let's tighten table turns this coming weekend. Pre-bus constantly, run food for each other.

Spring menu changes go live April 1. New dishes: asparagus risotto with Parmigiano crema, lamb ragu with hand-cut pappardelle, and strawberry-basil panna cotta. Full staff tasting Monday at 3 PM. Everyone needs to know the ingredients and allergen callouts cold.

Nakamura menu is locked — matcha tiramisu for dessert, 7 courses total. Tony, please get the specialty orders to Sysco (wagyu) and Capital Wine (sake pairings) by Monday at the latest. Lead times are tight.

Dr. Nakamura birthday dinner (April 5) — 7-course Japanese-Italian fusion tasting: dashi-infused burrata, miso black cod, yuzu risotto, wagyu tagliata, and matcha tiramisu finale. Tony and Robert at Capital Wine are coordinating specialty orders this week. This one is going to be special.
